attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches final acceleration in last 100-150m, excelling at timing over pure power. Tactically aggressive in chaos, willing to deviate lines and break sprint conventions in technical or disrupted finishes.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on Alpecin-Fenix leadout (especially van der Poel's kilometer-long pulls). Team excels at navigating crosswinds and positioning through crashes, maintaining organization when rival trains collapse. Less dependent on perfect leadout than traditional sprinters.
terrain_preference: Pan-flat long straights for acceleration timing. Surprisingly strong on technical finishes with chicanes/corners and cobbled classics. Crosswind splits favor him through team positioning. Struggles in extended 300m+ sprint drags where power endurance tested.
vulnerability: Gets boxed in technical finales under pressure, especially when contesting intermediate sprints. Fades in final 100m of extended sprints. Team chase organization occasionally fails. Cannot close minimal gaps at maximum speed against equal-caliber sprinters.
key_patterns: Won 4 consecutive Tour de France 2023 sprints, rare sustainability for pure sprinters
Repeatedly capitalizes when rival leadout trains crash or disorganize, superior chaos navigation (5+ instances)
Timing acceleration last 100m while rivals commit early, tactical restraint pattern across years
Strong crosswind positioning, makes front groups while rivals caught behind (Tirreno, Vuelta evidence)
Unexpected range: cobbles podium at Roubaix, top-10 TTs, drove selections at Brugge-De Panne
Boxing/positioning errors in technical finales when under pressure, repeated Vuelta/UAE issues
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Tour showed peak form with 4/4 sprint wins and sustainable speed, though third-place finish revealed speed ceiling against equal opposition. Recent classic podium shows expanding capabilities.
